name: FlashRAG
tagline: A Python toolkit for reproducible RAG research with 23 built-in algorithms and 36 benchmark datasets
seoTitle: FlashRAG: RAG Research Toolkit in Python
metaDescription: FlashRAG is a Python toolkit for RAG research. Reproduce 23 published RAG algorithms across 36 benchmark datasets with retrievers, rerankers, and generators.
overview: FlashRAG is a Python toolkit for reproducing and developing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) research. It bundles 36 pre-processed benchmark datasets and 23 published RAG algorithms behind one consistent interface, so you can re-run reported results or build your own pipeline without re-implementing each method from scratch.

It is aimed at researchers and engineers who compare RAG methods and need numbers they can trust. Because every method shares the same components for retrieval, reranking, generation, and compression, results across algorithms stay comparable and experiments are easier to reproduce.

As a RAG framework, FlashRAG focuses on the experiment loop rather than production serving. It includes preprocessing scripts for corpus handling and index building, and it uses tools like Faiss for vector search and vLLM for faster LLM inference.
features: 23 pre-implemented RAG algorithms with reported results, including 7 reasoning-based methods for multi-hop tasks
36 pre-processed benchmark RAG datasets for testing and validating model performance
Modular components for RAG pipelines: retrievers, rerankers, generators, and compressors
Preprocessing scripts for corpus processing, retrieval index building, and document pre-retrieval
Faster execution with vLLM and FastChat for LLM inference and Faiss for vector index management
A built-in UI to configure RAG baselines and run evaluation scripts visually

gettingStarted.intro: Install the package, then assemble a RAG pipeline from FlashRAG's config and pipeline classes.

steps.heading: Install FlashRAG
steps.body: Install the pre-release package from PyPI. You can also clone the repo and install from source.

steps.heading: Add optional acceleration
steps.body: For vLLM-accelerated inference and vector indexing, install the full extras and Faiss.
steps.code:
pip install flashrag-dev[full]
pip install vllm>=0.4.1
conda install -c pytorch faiss-cpu=1.8.0

steps.heading: Run a sequential pipeline
steps.body: Load a config and dataset, then run a SequentialPipeline with evaluation enabled.
steps.code:
from flashrag.config import Config
from flashrag.utils import get_dataset
from flashrag.pipeline import SequentialPipeline

config_dict = {'data_dir': 'dataset/'}
my_config = Config(
    config_file_path='my_config.yaml',
    config_dict=config_dict
)

all_split = get_dataset(my_config)
test_data = all_split['test']

pipeline = SequentialPipeline(my_config)
output_dataset = pipeline.run(test_data, do_eval=True)

useCases: Reproduce reported results for published RAG methods using the same datasets and settings
Benchmark several retrieval and generation strategies side by side on standard datasets
Prototype a custom RAG pipeline by mixing retrievers, rerankers, generators, and compressors
Study reasoning-based RAG methods on complex multi-hop question answering tasks
